description: Modify measurement region attributes. The ID must be specified to identify which measurement to modify. An error is thrown if no measurement region exists with the specified ID. Only specified attributes are changed; unspecified attributes retain their previous values. After modification, measurements must be recalculated with model clean.
notes: id keyword is REQUIRED - must specify which measurement to modify
Error thrown if specified ID does not exist
Only specified attributes are changed - others retain previous values
After modification, call model clean to recalculate with new parameters
Can modify multiple attributes in single command
Cannot change ID itself - use delete/create to reassign ID
More efficient than delete/create when keeping same ID
Position can be specified as vector (position) or components (x/y/z)
Modifying position or radius changes which bodies are in measurement region
Modifying tolerance affects porosity calculation accuracy
Modifying bins affects size distribution resolution
Python SDK likely has methods to modify measurement properties
Command interface provides simpler syntax for modifications
Exact Python method signatures - consult PFC documentation

command_vs_python.python:
# Python SDK approach - consult PFC documentation
# itasca.measure module provides modification functionality

workflow: 1. Initial measurement too small: measure create id 1 radius 2.0
2. Increase size: measure modify id 1 radius 5.0
1. Original location: measure create id 1 position (0,0,0)
1. Start with default: measure create id 1 radius 5.0
2. Need higher precision: measure modify id 1 tolerance 0.001
1. Initial coarse bins: measure create id 1 bins 50 0.0 1.0
2. Need finer resolution: measure modify id 1 bins 200 0.0 1.0
4. Export: measure dump for detailed distribution

delete_recreate.when_to_use: Complete reconfiguration or ID change needed

modify_vs_delete_recreate.recommendation: Use modify for parameter adjustments; delete/recreate only if changing ID

partial_modification.example: measure modify id 1 radius 6.0 leaves position, tolerance, bins unchanged
benefits: No need to re-specify all parameters
partial_modification.verification: Use measure list before and after to see what changed

recalculation_requirement.description: Measurements must be recalculated after modification
recalculation_requirement.why: Modification only changes configuration, not calculated values
recalculation_requirement.how: Call model clean to trigger recalculation
recalculation_requirement.values_before_recalculation: Old values remain until model clean called

component_form.example: measure modify id 1 x 5.0 y 3.0 z 2.0

partial_component.effect: Changes only x, keeps y and z unchanged

position_modification_methods.note: Cannot mix position vector and x/y/z components in same command - choose one form

attribute_preservation.description: Unspecified attributes retain their previous values
attribute_preservation.recommendation: Only specify what you want to change

warnings.message: id keyword is REQUIRED - command fails if omitted
warnings.mitigation: Always specify 'id <i>' when using measure modify

warnings.message: Error thrown if specified ID does not exist
warnings.mitigation: Use measure list to verify ID exists before modifying

warnings.message: Modifications don't take effect until model clean is called
warnings.mitigation: Always call model clean after measure modify to see updated results

warnings.message: Cannot modify the ID itself - use delete/create to change ID
warnings.mitigation: Delete old measurement and create new one if ID change needed

solutions: Check if id keyword specified - it's required
Verify ID exists: measure list
If ID doesn't exist, use measure create instead
Check syntax: measure modify id 1 radius 5.0

troubleshooting.issue: Modified attributes don't show up in measure list
solutions: Configuration changed but not recalculated
Call model clean to recalculate
Then measure list will show updated values
Modification only changes config, not results
Cannot modify ID itself
Use delete/create sequence:
measure delete range id 1
measure create id 2 <same config>
Or keep existing ID if not critical
Configuration changed but not recalculated
Call model clean to recalculate with new position
New position may contain same or different bodies
Verify with measure list after model clean

troubleshooting.issue: Want to reset all attributes to defaults
solutions: measure modify requires explicit values
Easier to delete and recreate: measure delete, measure create
Or manually specify all default values in modify

performance_considerations.no_recalculation: measure modify does NOT trigger recalculation
performance_considerations.recalculation_cost: model clean cost same as initial calculation
performance_considerations.recommendation: Can modify freely; recalculate only when needed

multiple_attribute_modification.description: Can modify multiple attributes in single command
multiple_attribute_modification.example: measure modify id 1 radius 4.0 x 2.0 y 2.0 tolerance 0.001
multiple_attribute_modification.recommendation: Modify all needed attributes in one command for efficiency

increase_tolerance.effect: Faster calculation, slightly less accurate porosity

tolerance_modification_effects.note: Must call model clean to see effect of tolerance change

bins_modification_effects.description: How bins setting affects size distribution
bins_modification_effects.note: Export with measure dump to see size distribution data

larger_radius.effect: Includes more bodies, averages over larger region

smaller_radius.effect: Includes fewer bodies, more localized measurement
smaller_radius.use_when: Want local properties at specific point

radius_modification_effects.note: Recalculate with model clean to see effect

keywords.name: bins
keywords.description: Modify the number of bins ( i ) and the lowest and highest ( fl and fh ) bin edges for the size distribution calculation. All bodies (e.g., clumps and balls) that fall within the measurement region are binned based on the volume (area) equivalent sphere (circle) diameters in 3D (2D). The cumulative volume percentage (between 0 and 1) of bodies as a function of bin size is calculated and can be exported to a table with the measure dump command. The table holds the bin boundaries, and the percentage for a bin is given with the entry holding the lower bin boundary.

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID.

keywords.name: radius
keywords.description: Radius of the measurement circle (sphere).

keywords.name: x
keywords.description: \(x\) -coordinate of the center of the measurement circle (sphere).

keywords.name: y
keywords.description: \(y\) -coordinate of the center of the measurement circle (sphere).

keywords.name: z
keywords.description: \(z\) -coordinate of the center of the measurement circle (sphere).

keywords.name: tolerance
keywords.description: percentage (range: p â‰¥ 0) for accumulating the volume of bodies that intersect the measurement region in the porosity calculation.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Position of the center of the measurement circle (sphere).

examples.description: Change radius of measurement 1 to 4.0, keep other attributes

examples.command: measure modify id 1 radius 4.0 x 2.0 y 2.0 z 2.0 tolerance 0.0
examples.description: Modify multiple attributes: radius, position, and tolerance
Move measurement 2 to new position using vector notation
Change only x-coordinate, keep y and z unchanged
Change size distribution to 200 bins from 0.0 to 1.0
examples.use_case: Increase resolution of particle size distribution

examples.command:
measure modify id 1 tolerance 0.001
model clean
measure list
examples.description: Increase precision, recalculate, view updated results

examples.command:
measure list
measure modify id 1 radius 6.0
model clean
measure list
examples.description: View before, modify, recalculate, view after
